items.title: Keppel DC REIT (KEPE.SI): 1H26 above: Strong earnings growth and balance sheet; Buy
items.summary: Keppel DC REIT (KDCREIT)'s 1H26 DPU of 5.71S¢ was +11.3% YoY and +8.9% HoH, above at 52% of full-year GSe and IBES consensus. While reversion was softer at 5%/10% for 2Q/6M26, forward renewal of Gore Hill should bring that up to low-teens in 3Q26.

items.title: Essential Properties Realty Trust (EPRT): 2Q26 First Take: Solid 2Q acquisitions and cap rates, with a FY guidance bump up (Buy)
items.summary: EPRT reported 2Q26 AFFOPS of $0.50, in line/-2.0% vs. GS/FactSet consensus estimate of $0.50/$0.51. More importantly, the Company completed $332.4mn of acquisitions in 2Q at an average cash/GAAP cap rate of 7.8%/9.1%, wh...

items.title: Link REIT (0823.HK): Divesting 50% stake of office tower in 100 Market Street Sydney at 6.5% NPI yield. Potential share repurchase up to 2%
items.summary: Before the market opened on July 22nd, Link REIT announced its disposal of 50% interest in an office tower at 100 Market Street Sydney to Aware Real Estate for A$225.9mn, based on the appraised value of the property as of end-May 2026.

items.title: Australia and New Zealand Economics Analyst: Launching Probabilistic Forecasts for Quarterly Trimmed Mean Inflation
items.summary: High inflation remains the key policy concern for the RBA. We launch probabilistic forecasts for quarterly trimmed mean inflation, the RBA’s preferred measure of underlying inflation, to help inform the near-term inflation outlook.

items.title: Americas Real Estate: REITs: REIT valuations screen more expensive after recent performance, reflecting a HALO market shift
items.summary: Given recent stock price movement, we take a quick look at US REIT valuations – at a sector level – in three ways: 1) FFO multiples, 2) equity risk premiums (ERPs), and 3) dividend yields. See inside for details.

items.title: Australia and New Zealand Economics Analyst: House Prices to Fall, Incomes to Stall, Spending to Slow
items.summary: A slowdown in household consumption growth is a key domestic macro risk to watch. We forecast consumer spending growth to decelerate 120bp to 1.3%yoy by end-2026, driven by a decline in household wealth and softer real income growth.

items.title: Terreno Realty Corp. (TRNO): 2Q26 Operating update: Occupancy & spreads above GS expectations
items.summary: TRNO released its 2Q26 operating update on July 8. Here we discuss the results and trends in detail and make minor adjustments to our model.

items.title: Australia and New Zealand Economics Analyst: Calibrating Our Nowcasts to Track Slower Consumption
items.summary: We forecast consumer spending growth to decelerate 120bp to 1.3%yoy by end-2026, driven by a decline in household wealth and softer real income growth. A steeper slowdown in household consumption growth is a key domestic macro risk to watch.
